summary: Closed, unmerged WIP attempt to replace ftwalljump.s with C. The PR also made broad supporting changes: a much more detailed fighter.h with partial Fighter, CollData, attr, physics, damage, callback, and flag layouts; a large fighter.c decompilation/update using tentative variable names and many offset-based field names; lbvector function renames and prototypes propagated through many asm callsites; added Dolphin math declarations; and updated obj_files.mk. The author explicitly said the wall-jump logic names were tentative and asked how to handle extern declarations involving HSD_GObj. Reviewer feedback said to declare externs in functions.h using `struct _HSD_GObj*` instead of the typedef to avoid circular include issues, and noted the branch needed a rebase because fighter changes conflicted with master. Evidence for the actual ftwalljump.c file is weak in the provided changed-file list/diff excerpt: the PR body says it replaces ftwalljump.s with ftwalljump.c, but no ftwalljump file appears in the slice's changed_files list or grep hits.
what_changed: PR body states the intended main change was replacing `ftwalljump.s` with `ftwalljump.c`, but this slice does not show a ftwalljump file in changed_files or diff grep results.

Expanded `src/melee/ft/fighter.h` from a placeholder `u32 data[0x222C / 4]` style Fighter layout into partial named structures, including `Vec2`, `Vec3`, `CollData`, a partial `Fighter`, embedded `attr`, physics fields, damage fields, callbacks, and bitflags.
Added wall-jump-related attribute names in fighter attributes: `x214_wall_jump_horizontal_velocity` and `x218_wall_jump_vertical_velocity`.
Added collision/environment field names for ground, right wall, left wall, and ceiling contact data in `CollData`, including normals/slopes and material/type bytes.

Added or updated a large fighter.c function body with offset comments, tentative names, calls to collision, stage, shield knockback, wind hazard, hitlag callback, death/offscreen, and position update helpers.

Renamed many lbvector symbols from lower-case or raw function names to more descriptive CamelCase suffixes, e.g. `lbvector_diff` to `lbvector_Diff`, `lbvector_normalize` to `lbvector_Normalize`, `lbvector_rotate` to `lbvector_Rotate`, `lbvector_add` to `lbvector_Add`, `lbvector_sub` to `lbvector_Sub`, `func_8000D8F4` to `lbvector_RotateAboutUnitAxis`, `func_8000D620` to `lbvector_Angle`, `func_8000D790` to `lbvector_AngleXY`, and `func_8000DC6C` to `lbvector_Mirror`.
Propagated lbvector symbol renames through many asm files in camera, fighter character files, items, stages, collision/library code, and text asm.
Added declarations to `include/dolphin/math.h` and touched `include/functions.h`; the author had left some externs local/commented due to HSD_GObj circular include concerns.
Updated `obj_files.mk` by one line, presumably to account for a new or changed C object in the WIP decompilation.
smart_moves: Used offset-preserving field names such as `x214_wall_jump_horizontal_velocity`, `x218_wall_jump_vertical_velocity`, `xf4_ground_attacker_shield_kb_vel`, and `x21d0_callback_EveryHitlag`, which make partial struct progress searchable while retaining exact layout evidence.
Kept uncertain game-logic names tentative and documented uncertainty in comments rather than overclaiming semantics.
Broke the opaque Fighter blob into targeted partial substructures for the fields used by the attempted decompilation, instead of requiring a fully solved Fighter struct before making progress.
Renamed lbvector helpers semantically and propagated the same symbol names into asm callsites, improving cross-file readability without changing branch targets/opcodes.
Annotated the decompiled fighter logic with assembly offset markers such as `//@39c`, `//@5ec`, and `//@738`, giving future matchers landmarks back to the original assembly.
Reviewer-suggested approach for circular includes was practical: use incomplete struct pointer declarations like `struct _HSD_GObj*` in prototypes rather than depending on typedef visibility.
decomp_lessons: For large fighter work, partial structs can be useful, but they are high-conflict areas; this PR was blocked partly because fighter changes conflicted with master and needed a rebase.
When declaring externs in common headers, prefer forward declarations/incomplete struct pointer types to avoid circular include dependencies.
Keep tentative field names offset-prefixed until behavior is verified; this makes the code useful without locking in bad semantics.
Broad symbol renames in low-level libraries like lbvector require updating many asm callsites, not just C headers, or the project will lose symbol consistency.

If a PR claims to replace an asm file with C, make sure the changed-file list and build file changes visibly include the new C file and removed/disabled asm file; in this slice the ftwalljump evidence is weak despite the title/body.
Large header expansions should be reviewed against current master early because fighter.h is central and likely to conflict with parallel decomp work.
naming_conventions: Offset-prefixed field names were used extensively for unknown or partially understood data: `x74_anim_vel`, `x80_self_vel`, `x8c_kb_vel`, `x594_anim_curr_flags_1`, `x18A4_knockbackMagnitude`, `x221c_7_hitstun`.
Known fields combine offsets with semantic labels, e.g. `x214_wall_jump_horizontal_velocity`, `x218_wall_jump_vertical_velocity`, `x128_ground_friction`, `x21d0_callback_EveryHitlag`.
Collision fields used descriptive contact-side names: `ground_normal`, `rightwall_slope`, `leftwall_slope`, `ceil_slope`, plus `*_index`, `*_info`, `*_type`, and `*_mat`.

lbvector symbols were renamed to `lbvector_` plus PascalCase operation names: `lbvector_Add`, `lbvector_Sub`, `lbvector_Diff`, `lbvector_Normalize`, `lbvector_Rotate`, `lbvector_Angle`, `lbvector_AngleXY`, `lbvector_Mirror`, `lbvector_Lerp`.
Reviewer-approved prototype convention for GObj in shared declarations: use `struct _HSD_GObj*` rather than relying on the typedef `HSD_GObj*` when avoiding include cycles.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Propagated C-side symbol renames to asm labels while preserving existing branch instruction bytes and addresses, e.g. `bl lbvector_diff` became `bl lbvector_Diff` at the same callsite.
Used assembly offset comments inside C to align decompiled blocks with original code positions.
Used explicit struct offsets and filler fields to map only needed portions of `Fighter` while preserving layout assumptions.
Used comments to explain compiler idioms, including integer-to-double conversion using `0x43300000_00000000` style constants.
Documented likely inlined or optimized-away behavior such as `fpclassify` checks and assert paths rather than pretending exact source was known.

Renamed raw functions to semantic lbvector helpers based on repeated call patterns, including rotations about unit axes, world-to-screen transforms, Euler rotations, angle calculations, mirror, and lerp.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Declare extern functions in `functions.h`; circular GObj include problems can be handled by declaring parameters as `struct _HSD_GObj*` and similar incomplete struct pointer types instead of using the typedef.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Rebase the branch because fighter changes were conflicting with master.

follow_up_queries: Find later merged PRs that completed `ftwalljump.c` or replaced `ftwalljump.s`.
Search for subsequent accepted Fighter/CollData struct definitions to compare against this WIP layout.
Search later PRs for `x214_wall_jump_horizontal_velocity` and `x218_wall_jump_vertical_velocity` to see whether these names survived.
Search for final accepted lbvector naming conventions after this PR, especially `lbvector_Diff`, `lbvector_Normalize`, and `lbvector_RotateAboutUnitAxis`.
Look for PRs resolving HSD_GObj circular include issues with `struct _HSD_GObj*` in `functions.h`.
